M-040.NAME: email-delivery
M-040.owns: Email provider adapter, verification email templates, provider error mapping, and safe operator evidence for email delivery.
M-040.purpose: Provide a KrotPN-native email delivery surface for verified registration without exposing tokens or provider secrets.

exports.fn-send-verification-email: Send a one-time registration verification link or code to a normalized email address.
exports.fn-build-verification-template: Render Russian and English subject, HTML, and text fallback content.
exports.fn-map-email-provider-error: Map provider HTTP or transport failures to typed application errors.

implementation-state.state-1: Phase-27 implemented provider-neutral requests, Resend and SMTP adapters, disabled-provider guard, templates, safe log markers, and fake-provider test coverage.
implementation-state.state-2: Live provider smoke remains operator-run only after EMAIL_PROVIDER and provider secrets are configured outside tests.

source-transfer.reuse: Resend request payload shape, readable UTF-8 templates, provider error normalization.
source-transfer.do-not-reuse: Standalone KPprotoN portal registration endpoint or DETS token storage.

constraints.constraint-1: Verification tokens must never appear in logs, traces, or provider error payloads.
constraints.constraint-2: Email sending failures block registration activation but do not create user, trial, VPN, or MTProto records.
constraints.constraint-3: Provider configuration remains env-backed through M-001 settings.

M-040.hotspot: H-026: Email delivery must not leak verification tokens, API keys, or raw provider responses
